verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What dream this is that you have dreamed about him I do not know.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Where have I said that he was defiled by communion with heretics at Alexandria?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Tell me the book, produce the letter: but you will find absolutely no such statement.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Yet with your wonted carelessness of statement or rather impudence of lying, which makes you imagine that every one will believe what you say, you add: “When you quoted a text of Scripture against him in so insulting a way that I do not dare to repeat it with my own mouth.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: You do not dare to repeat it because you can make the charge seem worse by keeping silence; and, because your accusation has no facts to rest upon, you simulate modesty, so that the reader may imagine that you are acting from consideration towards me, although your lies show that you do not consider your own soul.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What is this text of Scripture which is too shameful to proceed out of that most shameless mouth of yours?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What shameful thing, indeed, can you mention in the sacred books?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: If you are ashamed to speak, at any rate you can write it down, and then I shall be convinced of wantonness by my own words.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I might be silent on all other points, and I should still prove by this single passage how brazen is your effrontery.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: You know how little I fear your impeachment.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: If you produce the evidence with which you threaten me, all the blame which now rests on you will rest on me.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I gave my reply to you when I dealt with Vigilantius; for he brought the same charges against me which you bring first in the guise of friendly eulogy, afterwards in that of hostile accusation.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I am aware who it was that stirred up his ravings against me; I know your plots and vices; I am not ignorant of his simplicity which is proclaimed by every one.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Through his folly your hatred against me found an outlet for its fury; and, if I wrote a letter to suppress it, so that you should not be thought to be the only one who possesses a literary cudgel, that does not justify you in inventing shameful expressions which you can find in no part of my writings whatever.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: You must accept and confess the fact that the same document which answered his madness aroused also your calumnies.
